resources.string: Shoots 2 seconds after pressing the shutter button.
Use this application with SteadyShot of lens off.
You can activate the shutter by positioning your fingers in front of the Eyepiece sensors. Eyepiece sensors are found around the viewfinder. When active, you cannot use the viewfinder.
You can activate the shutter by positioning your fingers in front of the Eyepiece sensors.
The shutter will not be activated even though you position your fingers in front of the Eyepiece sensors.
To use Touchless Shutter, make sure FINDER/MONITOR is set to Auto before launching this application.
Expresses the redness of dusk scenes impressively.
The recommended exposure mode is A mode.
Sets whether to save only the effect image or the effect image and original images together.
Saves 2 original images and an effect image.
Restores all the setting values adjusted for the current theme to their defaults.
Restore all the setting values adjusted for the current theme to their defaults?
The exposure compensation dial is invalid on this application.\nSet the exposure compensation value with .
The aperture ring is invalid on this application.\nSet the aperture value with  when in exposure mode A or M.
You can shoot vivid shots of the blue sky and sunset, etc. that have different brightness.
Displays shooting tips for taking pictures with this application.

Expresses landscapes with different brightness levels into one picture. You can also independently control the color of the land and the sky. \nIt will shoot an HDR photo after separately setting the exposure and white balance for the land and the sky respectively.
Choose the composition of the photo, and adjust the exposure and white balance of the land area.
Press the  and enter the settings for adjusting the boundary between the land area and the sky area. \nYou can also adjust the exposure and white balance for the sky area.
Press the shutter button, and the shutter will be released twice automatically, creating the image with the effects applied.\nWhen you want to shoot in RAW, go to the Menu in the application.

- Stabilize the device with a tripod when shooting.
- Set "Distortion Compensation" to "Off" before using this application.
The application is not recommended to use with lenses for which the compensation cannot be set to "Off".
- This guide can be seen from the "Shooting Tips" in the menu.
Choose the composition of the photo, and adjust the exposure and white balance of the land area.\nEnter the settings for adjusting the sky area by pressing SKY SET.
Sets the effect of the sky area.
Adjust the boundary between the land area and the sky area, and the exposure/white balance of the sky area.
Sets the position of the boundary between the land area and the sky area.
Sets the defocus condition of the boundary between the land area and the sky area.
Set the exposure of the sky area.\nYou can also adjust the exposure compensation, ISO, shutter speed, and aperture.
Matches to the white balance of the land area.
Adjusts the boundary between the land area and the sky area of the image taken.
This function is disabled with the “Sky Area Settings”.
“White Balance” setting of the land area and the sky area has been changed to \"Flash\".
“White Balance” setting of the land area has been changed to \"Flash\".
“White Balance” setting of the sky area has been changed to \"Flash\".
Shoots image with only different exposures.
The image of the sky with the applied settings is taken first, followed by the image of the land with its applied settings.
The image of the land with the applied settings is taken first, followed by the image of the sky with its applied settings.
After exposure, sets whether or not to adjust the boundary between the land area and the sky area before recording the images.
After exposure, does not adjust the boundary between the land area and the sky area before recording the images.
After exposure, adjusts the boundary between the land area and the sky area while the shot image is being viewed before recording the images.
Sets the exposure of the sky area.
Sets the white balance of the sky area.
Press the  and enter the settings for adjusting the boundary between the land area and the sky area. \nYou can also adjust the exposure and white balance for the sky area.
Choose the composition of the photo, and adjust the exposure and white balance of the land area.\nEnter the settings for adjusting the sky area by pressing SKY SET.
Adjust the boundary between the land area and the sky area, then the exposure/white balance of the sky area, and then shoot.
- Stabilize the device with a tripod when shooting.\n- This guide can be seen from the \"Shooting Tips\" in the menu.
